children.value: This page documents the release history of UMA models, including new features, improvements, and bug fixes.
~50% faster, ~40% more accurate on Open Molecules test set, and expanded data coverage!
Leading to improvements across molecular systems:
relative improvement on biomolecules vs UMA-S-1.1
relative improvement on electrolytes vs UMA-S-1.1
for UMA-S with turbo mode compared to previous releases
Numerical and stability improvements for Hessians and phonons
More accurate diatomics and ionization potentials
— polymers (OPoly26), ionization potentials, small molecule clusters, dimers
UMA is built to easily scale up to multi-node, multi-GPU parallel inference with
Run MD with ASE and LAMMPS on large-scale systems with ns/day speeds:
UMA handles all parallelism—no need to manually configure LAMMPS, Kokkos, MPI, CUDA, etc.
Client–server Ray framework enables batched simulations:
over serial calculations for batches of small systems on a single H100 GPU
The first public release of UMA, introducing a unified model for atoms across multiple domains.
architecture for high parameter count with fast inference
